repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

origination.label: creator
origination.corpname: New York State College of Human Ecology. Cornell Nursery School

origination.label: creator
origination.corpname: New York State College of Home Economics. Department of Family Life

origination.label: creator
origination.corpname: New York State College of Home Economics. Department of Child Development and Family Relationships

origination.label: creator
origination.corpname: New York State College of Human Ecology. Department of Human Development and Family Studies

origination.label: creator
origination.corpname: New York State College of Human Ecology. Department of Human Development

did.physdesc: Administrative records, student records, articles, course materials and curricula, photographs, films, scrapbooks, books, toys.
did.abstract: Nursery School records, ca. 1920-1970; Nursery School administrative records, ca. 1920-1950; and Family Life class student reports, 1940s.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

bioghist.head: ORGANIZATIONAL HISTORY
bioghist.p: In 1925 the Department of Family Life was organized and in 1945 the name was changed to the Department of Child Development and Family Relationships. In 1969 the name changed to Human Development and Family Studies, and during the 1998-99 year it was shortened to Human Development.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Nursery School records, ca. 1920-1970; Nursery School administrative records, ca. 1920-1950; and Family Life class student reports, 1940s. Includes photographs of various activities in the Nursery School from 1920-1979, a scrapbook describing all aspects of the Nursery School in the 1930's, wooden puzzles produced by Milton Bradley that were presented yearly, and "Domecon" baby books from the 1920s. Also a film showing a nursery school, bathing cute baby; Library Tower; Students on quadrangle; alumni reunion; singing on steps of Goldwin Smith; feeding baby. about 1930, it is unclear if this film is from this collection and whether it shows the Cornell Nursery School.

relatedmaterial.head: RELATED MATERIALS
relatedmaterial.p: The films Practice Babies and Nursery School from this collection have been digitized and can be found on Cornell's Library Media Space website along with other Home Economics and related films.

arrangement.head: SERIES LIST
arrangement.p: Series I. Nursery School Student Records Boxes 1-25 and 37
Series III. Nursery School Information: Curriculum, Programs, Publications Box 24, Box 26- folders 14-52, Box 27
Series IV. Photographs and Films Boxes 28-31, 33 and 34
Series V. Scrapbook, Baby Books, Toys Boxes 31, 35 and 36

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Cornell Nursery School records, #23-13-2365. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

accessrestrict.head: Access Restrictions:
accessrestrict.p: Access to Boxes 1-25, Box 27 Folders 1-19 and 26-32, Box 32, and Box 37 restricted until 2082 or by permission from the University Archivist.

userestrict.head: Restrictions on Use:
userestrict.p: Due to the fragility and potential degradation of moving image and sound recordings, viewing and listening is limited to items that have been digitized. If an item is in another media format, you may request to have the item digitized for access. Information on ordering access copies may be found on the Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections webpage.|Audio visual media that has been digitized: F-178, F824.|Identity of students in the restricted boxes of the collection are not to be published or otherwise released in such form that any individual mentioned in the records can be identified.

controlaccess.corpname: New York State College of Human Ecology

did.unittitle: Series I: Nursery School - Student Records
not alphabetical and F-Gre from the 1920-1939
Parents Meetings, Monday Council Reports, Records on students who attended the nursery school
Student Histories of College students who enrolled in Family Life Courses-100, 220 and 330-arranged alphabetically
Observations of nursery school children done by students when Katherine Reeves was the Director
Series III: Nursery School Information: Curriculum, Programs, Publications- Partially Restricted
Child Training and Nursery School Annual Reports
Nursery school children observations and parent interviews
Plans for play group - summer school
Integrated Course - Economics of the Household
Affiliations Committee - New York State - Childhood Education (Inactive)
Records for Albany - Miss Beebe's Curriculum
The Association for Arts in Childhood
Seminar on the Young Child and the Family
Students interested in Assistantships in summer school laboratories
The Child - published by the Children's Bureau
Home Nursing and Home Hygiene - syllabus
Home Economics - Sr. High School Curriculum
Problems for Parent Educators - edited by Flora Thurston
Nursery School Staff - Records and Reports
Series IV: Nursery School: Photographs and Films
Folder from the Cornell Chapter of the American Association of University Professors and index cards with nursery school expenditures listed
Photograph album of Miss Reeves' students working with nursery school children

16 mm Films-Black and white film of back yard play group (Summer 1939) use DVD #142; one color film of backyard play group (summer of 1939) use DVD #145; Troy Studies - baby in high chair, playpen (1928) use DVD #143; film from D.D. Scarborough (October 1929) use DVD #144
Silent color film of students playing at the Cornell Nursery School. (Use DVD-1006 in same box.)
Silent color film of students playing at the Cornell Nursery School - copy of film F-824

p.extref: Click here to see a digitized version of the film.

did.unittitle: Various photographs and negatives and glass negatives of nursery school children

Use DVD-764. Film showing a nursery school, bathing cute baby; Library Tower; Students on quadrangle; alumni reunion; singing on steps of Goldwin Smith; feeding baby. About 1930, it is unclear if this film is from this collection and whether it shows the Cornell Nursery School.
Practice Babies - Domecon babies. (Use DVD-1010 in same box.)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

p.extref: Click here to see a digitized version of the film.

scopecontent.p: Home Economics students with babies and children.

did.unittitle: Practice Babies - Domecon babies - copy of Film F-178

Nursery School Activities Photographs (10 of them) : Childhood Education September Issue; Lunchtime - April 20, 1926 with Karl Von Bug, Rodney Kearl, David Levine, Janet Carson, Rand Wilson, Elizabeth Wilson, Steven Schwartz and Jeanne Specht (2 copies); Tarri Balhell, Lynne Munschauer, Karl Von Bug, Rodney Kearl at the Nursery School, May 23, 1957; Kim O'Conner, Rodney Kearl and Scott Partridge, May 24, 1957; Lynne Munschauer, Jacie Balliett, Patricia Hughes, Jean Anderson (participating student), Peter Campbell, Janet Corson and Steven Schwartz
Nursery School Children - 18 photographs, including Peter Racedalph
Nursery School Activities - various years: 20 photographs, including 15 contact sheets
Nursery School Activities - various years: 8 photographs, 2 photos dated July 9, 1970
Outdoor Activities - 13 photographs including photographs of playground equipment from Child-Life and Play Speciality Association, Hollistor, Massachusetts

Outdoor Activities - 28 photos, including Lois Ann at 2½ thumb tacking her paper to an easel and a photo of George Washington Chang at the Nursery School, that was used in the Post Standard, July 16, 1944
Outdoor Activities: 24 photos, including John Leete Stule and Ann Leete Wheeler, riding tricycles
Nursery School Groups of students and teachers: 18 photos
Nursery School Hygiene: various years, 16 photos
Photos of Jennifer Birckmayer, Director of the Nursery School
Series V: Scrapbook, Baby Books, Toys
Information About the twins, Mary Alice and Rose Ann and Baby Sheila
Oversized scrapbook describing the early days of the Nursery School
Wooden gift toys and tru-tone, no-roll crayons all produced by the Milton Bradley Company
